text:
There once was a hare who fell in love. Her name was Heather. And every day she would hop through the forest, singing hymns that soared up to the branches. One day, a crow swooped down from a towering oak. The crow listened to Heather's song, fell in love, and decided to join her.

Heather laughed. "Oh, how I long to soar with you through the autumn leaves!"

"And you could," said the crow. "We could flap them right off the trees!"

Heather's heart ached. "No," she said. "I want to save those leaves for another day. For now, I sing my songs."

With those words, the crow took to the skies. Heather never forgot the birds' laughter. Each spring, when wildflowers bloomed, Heather would fill the air with music. But something in her melodies grew lonely; the songs were melodies she hadn’t known in so long.

Decades later, Heather found the crow.
